frameworks.name: compositional generalization
frameworks.one_line: recomposing learned functions to solve unseen problems

frameworks.name: Hebbian gating neural network
frameworks.one_line: gating mechanism captures curriculum effects on transfer

frameworks.name: standard neural networks
frameworks.one_line: fail at the compositional transfer humans achieve

core_question: How do training curricula shape humans' ability to compositionally recompose learned functions for novel problems?
key_assumptions: transfer can be formalized as recomposition of existing functions
training regime structure (axis alignment, temporal correlation) is a key determinant
human-style gating can be modelled with Hebbian mechanisms
manipulations: temporal correlation of items during training
comparison of human learners against standard neural network baselines
key_findings: humans generalize compositionally where standard neural networks fail
generalization improves when training items are axis-aligned and temporally correlated
adults learn composable functions asynchronously with developmental-like discontinuities
interpretations: a Hebbian gating process can account for how curriculum shapes compositional transfer
asynchronous, staged acquisition of components parallels child-development learning
